topic: Accelerate agentic tool calling with serverless model customization in Amazon SageMaker AI
objective: Explain, as part 2 of the mcp-deep-dive series ('MCP in Action: Real-World Applications and Case Studies'), how hosting a customized (fine-tuned) tool-calling model on a serverless Amazon SageMaker AI endpoint reduces cost and latency for MCP-based agentic systems, and demonstrate the underlying tool-calling protocol with a runnable local example.

claims.statement: MCP was launched by Anthropic on November 25, 2024, as an open-standard protocol for AI-data integration.
MCP reduces integration complexity from O(N*M) bespoke connectors to O(N+M) standardized interfaces between AI applications and tools/data sources.
MCP is built on the JSON-RPC 2.0 specification, so messages are language-agnostic between clients and servers.
MCP defines three core primitives exposed by a server: Resources (static/queryable data), Tools (executable functions), and Prompts (reusable templates).
MCP supports STDIO transport for local processes (newline-delimited JSON-RPC messages on stdin/stdout) and HTTP with Server-Sent Events (SSE) for remote connections.

AWS describes an approach for hosting agentic tool-calling models with serverless/on-demand model customization on Amazon SageMaker AI, aimed at accelerating and reducing the cost of the tool-selection step in agent loops compared to always-on dedicated GPU endpoints.
Amazon SageMaker AI is AWS's managed platform for building, training, fine-tuning, and deploying machine learning models, including inference options that scale down between bursts of traffic.
Standardized server tooling for exposing model-facing endpoints (e.g. Gradio's MCP/server integration) is an active trend making it easier to wrap existing apps as agent-callable tools.
